Title: Structural Insights into the Substrate Recognition of Ginsenoside Glycosyltransferase Pq3-O-UGT2

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 3-O-glycan chain extension of protopanaxadiol-type ginsenosides; also flavonoid glycoside glycosylation side activity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to the C2-OH of the C3-linked glucose moiety of ginsenoside Rh2 to form ginsenoside Rg3, and of ginsenoside F2 to form ginsenoside Rd; also catalyzes β(1→2) glycosylation of the C3-O-glucose of liquiritin to form liquiritin-2′-O-glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: UDP-glucose; ginsenoside Rh2; ginsenoside F2; liquiritin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal product-determining glycosylation step for conversion of Rh2 to rare ginsenoside Rg3; not explicitly established as rate-limiting in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of ginsenoside Rg3 and Rd, with wild-type enzyme showing higher catalytic efficiency toward F2 than Rh2; engineered variants can shift selectivity toward Rh2/Rg3 production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: X-ray crystal structures of apo and substrate-bound complexes; site-directed mutagenesis; enzyme activity assays; kinetic analysis; docking; HPLC/LC-MS product profiling; NMR for liquiritin product
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter in pET-28a expression system; inducible bacterial expression
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 plant UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase (UGT); GT-B fold; PSPG motif-containing glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ginsenoside Rg3 is described as a rare ginsenoside and primary component of traditional Chinese antitumor drugs; ginsenosides/flavonoid glycosides have health benefits
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli Rosetta (DE3) for protein production
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assays with UDP-glucose at 37 °C; crystallization/co-crystallization with Rh2 or F2
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Wild-type enzyme preferentially converts F2 over Rh2, which is unfavorable for selective Rg3 production; some multi-site mutations compromise Rh2 catalytic efficiency
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Crystal/PDB entries reported for apo and complexes: 8JZQ, 8K08, 8K09. Structural analysis revealed an Nα5-oriented acceptor pocket and crucial Nα5-Nα6 linker conformation. Hydrophobic interactions dominate Rh2 recognition; both hydrophobic contacts and an F2-specific hydrogen-bonding contribution are involved in F2 recognition. The enzyme shows limited promiscuity toward other ginsenosides but can glycosylate liquiritin.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pq3-O-UGT2 is the central glycosyltransferase experimentally studied and directly converts Rh2 to Rg3 and F2 to Rd using UDP-glucose. 2. Crystal structures showed a distinctive Nα5-oriented hydrophobic acceptor-binding pocket whose formation depends on the Nα5-Nα6 linker conformation. 3. His20-Asp116 acts as the catalytic dyad, while Asp359 and Gln360 in the PSPG motif are essential for donor binding/catalysis. 4. Hydrophobic residues lining the pocket determine substrate recognition and selectivity, and F2 recognition additionally involves a specific Ser175-linked hydrogen-bonding contribution. 5. Engineering mutations, especially S175A/L181W, increased catalytic efficiency and selectivity toward Rh2, supporting application for Rg3 bioproduction.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High value for enzyme engineering and biomanufacturing of rare ginsenoside Rg3. The S175A/L181W variant is a promising catalyst for in vitro production systems; additional optimization of thermostability and immobilization was suggested. More suitable for enzymatic manufacturing than plant breeding in this study context.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 3-O-glycan chain extension of Rh2 toward Rg3
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Engineered variant of Pq3-O-UGT2 that transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to Rh2 more efficiently and more selectively than to F2
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product and shifts flux preference toward Rg3 production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Engineered key catalytic variant for selective Rg3 production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased catalytic efficiency toward Rh2 to 126.40 s-1 mM-1 (~4.43-fold of wild type) while reducing efficiency toward F2 to 54.29 s-1 mM-1, giving ~2-fold preference for Rh2 over F2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Site-directed mutagenesis; purified enzyme assays; Michaelis-Menten kinetic analysis; HPLC product quantification
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: T7 promoter in pET-28a expression system; inducible bacterial expression
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Engineered family 1 plant UDP-glycosyltransferase (GT-B fold, PSPG motif-containing)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rare ginsenoside; primary component of traditional Chinese antitumor drugs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in E. coli Rosetta (DE3)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro kinetics at 37 °C with UDP-glucose
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Engineered specificity was beneficial, but broader multi-site combinations in different regions often reduced Rh2 activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ser175 and Leu181 are both located near the N-terminal end of the Nα6 helix, a region sensitive to sugar acceptor differences. This mutant was highlighted by the paper as especially promising for industrial Rg3 production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The S175A/L181W double mutant was the best engineered variant reported for Rh2-selective catalysis. 2. It improved Rh2 catalytic efficiency beyond L181W alone and further lowered F2 efficiency. 3. The result demonstrates that rational engineering of pocket residues can alter both activity and substrate selectivity for rare ginsenoside production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent metabolic engineering and biocatalysis value for in vitro manufacture of ginsenoside Rg3; suitable candidate for further thermostability improvement and immobilized enzyme platforms.
